what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that lays out a buildable specification for a whole-repository, coverage-complete code-archaeology dragnet, including its output store, record schema, coverage ledger, change handling, model usage, synthesis gating, and execution plan, plus an amendment that elevates it to a reusable build-prep primitive and adds hardening requirements.
observations: It is a request-type noticeboard item in the provider-registry channel, filed by session ch-3mpkjg3r and currently open.
It mandates a coverage-complete, tiered, queryable, incremental dragnet over code files, using the FsStore space code_archaeology.
Each file record is addressed at code://<rel_path> and the coverage ledger at code://_ledger; the amendment changes addressing to project-first code://<project>/<rel_path> with a run version key.
Records are dual: embedded for semantic query and structured with Pydantic for field query, split into coarse and fine tiers.
The coverage ledger must enumerate every file from the real filesystem tree, account for every file in states, and fail loudly if the denominator does not match the sum of states.
Re-runs are incremental via per-file sha256 fingerprints; only changed or new files are re-described.
Execution proposes using 32-concurrent local chat-4b via run_items, and warns of contention with a corpus bake already firing.
Synthesis and provider-registry decisions are explicitly gated until full coverage is proven.

The amendment treats the dragnet as a reusable build-prep primitive, adds parser-first deterministic extraction, requires a ProjectionRegistry space registration, a structured field-index, and manifest hygiene.
It explicitly rejects git-ls-files as the coverage denominator, citing git-ignored directories such as reference/ and source/ that contain the actual design-system foundation.
standouts: Coverage-correctness rule: the denominator is the real filesystem tree, including git-ignored content directories, not git-ls-files.
Elevation from a provider-registry one-off to a reusable, cross-project build-prep primitive, with discipline to prove it on the company repo first.
Fail-loud ledger accounting: denominator must equal the sum of all per-file states, with no silent drops.
Parser-first extraction: deterministic structural facts from AST/regex, reserving the LLM for neutral prose only.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter convention with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, history, and timestamps.
Uses bullet lists and numbered sections to organize a technical specification inside a board item.
Includes an explicit amendment section marked with a fork date and hardening requirements.
References external artifacts by address, such as build-prep/the-one-application/CODE-ARCHAEOLOGY-DRAGNET-DESIGN.md and board://item-9b12c1af.
Ends with a metadata note that there are no code symbols in the file.
concerns: The title is extremely long and dense with parentheticals, making scanning harder.
A single board item mixes the original request, detailed design, and a substantial amendment, which may complicate version tracking.
It references external design documents and other board items without including their contents, so the full picture is not self-contained.
Execution is explicitly held pending confirmations, so the spec has not yet been implemented or validated.
Heavy use of all-caps emphasis and special markers creates visual noise in a structured record.
notes: A provider-registry noticeboard request that specs a whole-repo, coverage-complete code-archaeology dragnet and an amendment promoting it as a reusable build-prep primitive, pending Tim's confirm and chat-4b contention clearance.
questions: What is the exact current state of the corpus bake and chat-4b contention mentioned in the execution section?
What are the precise validation rules and tooling for the Pydantic coarse/fine record schemas?
How does the real-filesystem enumerator distinguish true junk from content directories, and where is that exclusion policy recorded?
What is the project identifier that will be used in code://<project>/<rel_path> for the company repo?
What is the relationship between this dragnet and the existing dragnet_extract.py / corpus extraction engine?
What is the content of board://item-9b12c1af and the referenced design doc, and how do they interact with this spec?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file presents itself as a buildable spec for a whole-repo code-archaeology dragnet and does exactly that, including the later amendment that hardens and elevates the design.
apparent_themes: Descriptive neutrality and avoidance of premature architectural judgment
Fail-loud accounting and incremental change handling
intention_signals: The underlying idea is to stop agents from sampling the codebase, getting confident, and building from partial information by first producing a complete, queryable, neutral map of every file, then using that map to ground provider-registry and architectural decisions.
novelty: It explicitly rejects the common git-ls-files denominator in favor of the real filesystem tree to capture git-ignored foundation content, and it pairs deterministic parser-based structural extraction with LLM-generated neutral prose inside a tiered, dual-queryable record store.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, actionable blueprint for a coverage-complete code-archaeology dragnet, including record schemas, a fail-loud coverage ledger, addressing conventions, incremental re-run logic, and hardening amendments, which a fused system could keep as the specification for a reusable pre-build mapping primitive.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard request item item-d1a7bf75 in the provider-registry channel that specifies a whole-repo, coverage-complete, tiered, queryable, incremental code-archaeology dragnet with dual embedded/structured records, a fail-loud coverage ledger, and an amendment elevating it to a reusable build-prep primitive.
intention_for_embedding: To prevent partial-information architectural decisions by first mapping every file in the real filesystem, then synthesizing provider-registry choices from complete coverage.
